trivia_bullets.text: Duskmourn: House of Horror (DSK, released September 27, 2024) is set inside a sentient mansion-plane ruled by the demon Valgavoth, who expanded his prison to swallow an entire plane. The set is top-down inspired by 1970s-1990s horror tropes: slasher films, haunted houses, demonic pacts. [Scryfall], [Wizards Planeswalker's Guide to Duskmourn]

Flavor text (verbatim): "Elsa swelled with euphoria and power as she stepped over her friends' corpses. She should have bargained with a demon a lot sooner." The named character Elsa is an original Duskmourn survivor NPC — not a named recurring planeswalker — whose arc of crossing a moral threshold for power mirrors the card's mechanical function (digging for the card you need at the cost of milling the rest). [Scryfall]

Commune with Evil ({2}{B}) is a {3} CMC black sorcery that impulse-draws from four cards (pick one, mill three) while gaining 3 life. Functionally similar to Read the Bones and Blood Pact but without the scry; the life gain offsets the card disadvantage. EDHREC rank 10,968 among DSK cards — mid-tier Commander adoption, likely used in self-mill and reanimator shells. [Scryfall], [EDHREC via WebSearch snippet]

Artist Ovidio Cartagena illustrated this card. Cartagena is a Colombian illustrator who has worked on multiple DSK cards; no specific designer commentary on this card's commission was located in available sources. [Scryfall artist field]
